DISCOVERY 301, RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO HAVE THE TRUTH OF MATTERS REQUESTED FOR ADMISSION TO SEAMUS COLLINS CONSTRUCTION INC BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS. Off Calendar Jun-02-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Construction Inc Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL THOMAS' Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Construction Inc Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Prior to the hearing on Michael Thomas's motion for deemed-admission orders as to his requests for admissions served on Seamus Collins Construction Inc. (SCCI), defendant SCCI served responses. The court continued the matter for hearing and now concludes that the responses were substantially compliant. The court denies the request for a deemed-admitted order. (See CCP 2033.280(c).) Sanctions in this circumstance are mandatory and the court orders SCCI to remit sanctions of $910 payable to Thomas within 20 days of entry of this order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL THOMAS' To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Prior to the hearing on Michael Thomas's motion for deemed-admission orders as to his requests for admissions served on Seamus Collins, defendant Collins served responses. The court continued the matter for hearing and now concludes that the responses were substantially compliant. The court denies the request for a deemed-admitted order. (See CCP 2033.280(c).) Sanctions in this circumstance are mandatory and the court orders Collins to remit sanctions of $2660 payable to Thomas within 20 days of entry of this order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have The Truth Of Matters Requested For Admission To Seamus Collins Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
